content:
# Revelation Chapter 19 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 291-292 | **Source Image:** REV_019_page_001.jpg, REV_019_page_002.jpg

1. After these things I heard something like a loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, saying, "Hallelujah! The salvation and the glory and the power belong to our God,

2. for true and righteous are his judgments; for he has judged the great harlot who was corrupting the earth with her fornication, and he has avenged the blood of his servants from her hand."

3. And a second time they said, "Hallelujah! And her smoke rises up forever and ever."

4. And the twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshiped God who sits upon the throne, saying, "Amen. Hallelujah!"

5. And a voice came from the throne, saying, "Praise our God, all his servants, and those who fear him, the small and the great."

6. And I heard something like the voice of a great multitude and like the sound of many waters and like the sound of mighty thunders, saying, "Hallelujah! For the Lord our God, the Almighty, reigns.

7. Let us rejoice and be glad and give the glory to him, for the marriage of the Lamb has come, and his wife has made herself ready."

8. And it was given to her to clothe herself in fine linen, bright and clean; for the fine linen is the righteous deeds of the saints.

9. And he says to me, "Write: 'Blessed are those who are called to the marriage supper of the Lamb.'" And he says to me, "These are the true words of God."

10. And I fell before his feet to worship him. And he says to me, "See that you do not do that; I am a fellow servant of yours and of your brothers who hold the testimony of Jesus. Worship God. For the testimony of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy."

11. And I saw heaven opened, and behold, a white horse, and the one sitting upon it is called Faithful and True, and in righteousness he judges and makes war.

12. And his eyes are like a flame of fire, and upon his head are many diadems, having a name written that no one knows except himself,

13. and he is clothed in a garment dipped in blood, and his name is called The Word of God.

14. And the armies that are in heaven were following him on white horses, clothed in fine linen, white and clean.

15. And from his mouth comes a sharp sword, so that with it he may strike the nations; and he will shepherd them with a rod of iron; and he treads the winepress of the wrath of the anger of God the Almighty.

16. And he has on his garment and on his thigh a name written: "King of kings and Lord of lords."

17. And I saw one angel standing in the sun, and he cried out with a loud voice, saying to all the birds that fly in mid-heaven, "Come, gather together for the great supper of God,

18. so that you may eat the flesh of kings and the flesh of commanders and the flesh of mighty men, and the flesh of horses and of those who sit upon them, and the flesh of all men, both free and slave, and small and great."

19. And I saw the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies gathered together to make war against the one who sits upon the horse and against his army.

20. And the beast was seized, and with it the false prophet who performed the signs before it, by which he deceived those who had received the mark of the beast and those who worshiped its image. These two were thrown alive into the lake of fire that burns with sulfur.

21. And the rest were killed with the sword that came from the mouth of the one who sits upon the horse, and all the birds were filled with their flesh.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

- **19:1 "Hallelujah" (ΑΛΛΗΛΟΥΙΑ):** Transliteration of the Hebrew retained in the Greek text. This word appears four times in this chapter (vv. 1, 3, 4, 6) and nowhere else in the New Testament.
- **19:8 "righteous deeds" (ΔΙΚΑΙΩΜΑΤΑ):** Plural form indicating specific righteous acts/ordinances of the saints, not righteousness as an abstract quality.
- **19:10 "fellow servant" (ΣΥΝΔΟΥΛΟΣ):** The angel identifies himself as a co-slave alongside John and his brothers, emphasizing that worship belongs to God alone.
- **19:13 "dipped" (ΒΕΒΑΜΜΕΝΟΝ):** Sinaiticus reads ΒΕΒΑΜΜΕΝΟΝ ("dipped/dyed"), a perfect passive participle. Some later manuscripts read ΕΡΡΑΝΤΙΣΜΕΝΟΝ ("sprinkled") or ΠΕΡΙΡΕΡΑΜΜΕΝΟΝ ("sprinkled around"). The Sinaiticus reading aligns with the imagery of Isaiah 63:1-3.
- **19:13 "The Word of God" (Ο ΛΟΓΟΣ ΤΟΥ ΘΕΟΥ):** The title from which this translation takes its name. A direct identification of the divine warrior with the pre-existent Logos of John 1:1.
- **19:15 "wrath of the anger" (ΤΟΥ ΘΥΜΟΥ ΤΗΣ ΟΡΓΗΣ):** Two distinct Greek words for divine wrath used together for intensification — ΘΥΜΟΣ (burning fury) and ΟΡΓΗ (settled indignation).
- **19:16 word order:** "King of kings and Lord of lords" (ΒΑΣΙΛΕΥΣ ΒΑΣΙΛΕΩΝ ΚΑΙ ΚΥΡΙΟΣ ΚΥΡΙΩΝ) — the reverse order from 17:14, forming an inclusio across these chapters.
- **19:20 "lake of fire":** First occurrence of ΛΙΜΝΗ ΤΟΥ ΠΥΡΟΣ in Revelation; it becomes the final destination of evil in chapters 20-21.

-- LOGOS
